name: Oral nicotinamide did not reduce keratinocyte cancers in transplant recipients
summary: A later phase 3 trial limits generalization of nicotinamide chemoprevention to immunosuppressed transplant recipients.
statement: In solid-organ transplant recipients, 500 mg nicotinamide twice daily did not reduce new keratinocyte cancers or actinic keratoses over 12 months.
population_or_model: Immunosuppressed solid-organ transplant recipients with prior keratinocyte cancers
quantitative_note: The trial reported 207 new keratinocyte cancers in the nicotinamide group and 210 in placebo, rate ratio 1.0 (95% CI 0.8 to 1.3; P=0.96).
caveats: The trial was stopped early because of poor recruitment.
The transplant-recipient population is immunosuppressed and may not generalize to immunocompetent high-risk adults.
The null result constrains broad claims about nicotinamide as a skin-cancer prevention or DNA-repair intervention.
